item.title: Ask the forge: your light pollution questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the light pollution questions backyard astrophotographers keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in targets with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest targets adjustments that backyard astrophotographers report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: stacking software guide
item.description: The persistent myths around stacking software guide, stress-tested against what backyard astrophotographers actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your rigs in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying rigs project for backyard astrophotographers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/AskAstrophotography is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the light pollution community this week.

item.title: The targets checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified targets purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of processing
item.description: The most common early stumbles in processing, and the cheap corrections that fix them for backyard astrophotographers.

item.title: "astrophotography without tracker" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about astrophotography without tracker through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.
